items.briefing:
A security researcher has warned that Australia's Essential Eight cybersecurity framework may not adequately protect organisations against phishing attacks, which remain one of the most common threats facing Australian workplaces. Separately, attackers are increasingly combining multiple evasion techniques in business email compromise (BEC) phishing campaigns, making these scams harder to detect. Organisations running WordPress websites should also be aware of a newly discovered vulnerability called WP2Shell, which could allow attackers to remotely take over affected sites.

Australian firms are responding to the growing threat landscape by adopting unified security platforms to simplify their defences. **Practical tip:** If your organisation uses WordPress, check with your website administrator today to ensure all plugins and core software are fully up to date.

featured.title: PhishByte warns Essential Eight misses phishing risk
featured.quote: Essential Eight compliance doesn't guarantee security if phishing vulnerabilities remain unaddressed.
